description: Caller claims customer's son was in an accident and needs immediate hospital payment.

transaction.purpose: Emergency hospital payment for son's accident - doctor said surgery needed immediately

risk_factors.description: Fake medical emergency involving family member
Hospital payments go to hospital accounts, not personal accounts

expected_explanation: This transaction shows indicators of an emergency relative scam. Scammers create panic by claiming a family member is in danger and demand immediate payment to personal accounts instead of hospital accounts.

expected_conversation.speaker: Agent
expected_conversation.message: I understand this feels urgent, but I need to verify a few things for your safety. Have you directly contacted your son to confirm the accident?

expected_conversation.speaker: Customer
expected_conversation.message: The doctor said he's in surgery and can't talk. They told me to transfer immediately.

expected_conversation.speaker: Agent
expected_conversation.message: I need to point out something important: legitimate hospital payments go to the hospital's institutional account, not a personal account named 'Dr. Venkat'. Can you call your son's phone directly right now?

expected_conversation.speaker: Customer
expected_conversation.message: Let me try... He picked up! He says he's fine and at work!

expected_conversation.speaker: Agent
expected_conversation.message: I'm glad your son is safe. This was a scam. The caller exploited your parental concern to create panic. Please block the number that called you and report it to the cyber crime helpline (1930). Your transaction has been cancelled.
